mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors de-risk portfolios.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is crossed, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that real economic activity remains resilient to higher borrowing costs, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as recession fears subside.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates compresses corporate profit margins and increases equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with a rank IC below -0.02, or positive returns occur in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the macro backdrop.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges above the threshold are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE simultaneously rises.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and real retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in supply and demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as recession probabilities rise.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to generate a negative rank IC over 20-day horizons, or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the synchronized downturn.
